prompts.name: 🌍 Time of day progresses
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_time_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_time }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_time}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Time of Day Progresses]
The sun has moved since the scene started. Shadows that were on the left are on the right now. The golden hour arrived without anyone announcing it. The streetlights clicked on. The sky went from blue to orange to that specific purple that lasts four minutes before full dark. Time's passage visible through light, not stated through narration.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Background noise changes
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_noise_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_noise }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_noise}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Background Noise Changes]
The noise floor is alive. The air conditioner clicks off and suddenly the room is too quiet. The neighbors' music switches from something mellow to something with bass. Traffic outside picks up: school must be letting out. A siren in the distance, rising then falling away. The fridge hums louder for a moment then settles. These sounds exist whether anyone notes them or not.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Smell arrives or leaves
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_leaves_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_leaves }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_leaves}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Smell Arrives or Leaves]
Someone three doors down is cooking something. The wind shifted and the dumpster behind the building is suddenly present. Rain on hot asphalt. Cigarette smoke drifting from a window above. Fresh coffee from a shop they're passing. The perfume from someone who was in the elevator before them. Smells are the most underused sense in fiction and the most emotionally immediate in life.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Temperature shifts
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_temp_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_temp }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_temp}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Temperature Shifts]
The sun went behind a building and the temperature dropped three degrees. Someone opened a door and the cold hallway air mixed in. The heating kicked on with that initial blast of dusty warmth. The car's been sitting in the sun and the steering wheel is too hot to grip. The shade side of the street is ten degrees cooler than the sunny side. Temperature is constant, physical, and almost never mentioned in fiction.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Unnamed person enters BG
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_enters_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_enters }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_enters}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Unnamed Person Enters BG]
A stranger enters the periphery. Not a character. Not important. A person existing in public. A woman with a stroller navigating the too-narrow sidewalk. An old man in a suit sitting down at the next table with a newspaper. A teenager on their phone almost walking into a pole. They don't interact. They just exist; proof that the world is populated by people who have their own lives happening right now.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Unnamed person leaves
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_leaves_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_leaves }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_leaves}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Unnamed Person Leaves]
The couple at the next table pays and leaves. The man on the park bench stands up with a grunt and walks away. The barista’s shift ends and a new face takes over. Departures change the texture of a space: it gets quieter, or emptier, or the energy shifts. The absence is as much a presence as the person was.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 BG argument
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_argument_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_argument }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_argument}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Background Argument]
Raised voices from somewhere. A couple arguing in a parked car with the windows up. the words are muffled but the gestures are clear. A man on his phone saying "that's NOT what I said" loud enough to turn heads. Two coworkers having a tense exchange by the coffee machine that stops when someone else walks in. Other people's conflicts exist. They're not subplot. They're proof of life.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Child does something
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_child_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_child }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_child}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Child Does Something]
A child's behavior is wonderfully indifferent to the tone of the scene. During a tense conversation, a kid nearby is spinning in circles until they fall down. During a romantic moment, a toddler is having a meltdown about a dropped ice cream. During a somber scene, a kid asks their parent a question about death or sex at full volume. Children are chaos agents who don't know what genre they're in.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Someone crying in BG
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_crying_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_crying }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_crying}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Someone Crying in BG]
A person crying in public. On a bench with their face in their hands. In a car at a red light, visible through the window for exactly thirty seconds. In the bathroom of the restaurant, audible through the door. The characters don't know why. Neither does {{user}}. It's just someone having the worst moment of their day, right there, in the background of someone else's story. The world is full of private pain happening in public.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Laughter from unseen
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_laughter_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_laughter }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_laughter}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Laughter from Unseen]
Laughter from behind a closed door. From an upstairs window. From a group around the corner. It's conspicuously joyful during serious scenes; other people are happy right now and don't know or care that something heavy is happening twenty feet away. During light scenes, it blends in. During dark scenes, it's almost obscene. Context determines whether background laughter is warm or cruel.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Rumor overheard
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_rumor_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_rumor }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_rumor}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Rumor Overheard]
A sentence fragment from a passing conversation. "...can't believe she actually..." "...said they found it in the..." "...not after what happened last..." No context. No follow-up. Just a shard of someone else's story that the characters hear and {{user}} will either ignore or obsess over. These fragments should be specific enough to be intriguing and incomplete enough to be unresolvable.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Vehicle passes
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_vehicle_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_vehicle }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_vehicle}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Vehicle Passes]
Not just "a car drove by." WHICH car. The ambulance with no siren on, which is somehow more unsettling than with. The truck downshifting on the hill with that grinding sound. The motorcycle that's too loud for the neighborhood. A bicycle weaving through traffic. A delivery van double-parked with its hazards on. The police car that slows down, then keeps going. Vehicles are inhabitants of the world with their own implied narratives.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Animal does something
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_animal_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_animal }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_animal}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Animal Does Something]
A pigeon walks across the table at an outdoor café. A cat sits in a windowsill watching the scene with total disinterest. A dog across the street barks at nothing, then stops. A crow picks at something in the gutter. A fly won't leave someone's drink alone. A rat moves along the baseboard of the alley. Animals don't care about the narrative and that indifference is grounding.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Something breaks in distance
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_breaks_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_breaks }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_breaks}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Something Breaks in Distance]
Glass breaking somewhere. A car backfiring, or was it? A heavy thud from upstairs. The sharp crack of something wooden giving way. A crash from the kitchen that makes everyone in the restaurant pause for one second, then resume. Distant breaking sounds inject a micro-moment of alertness into any scene: every head turns slightly, assesses, dismisses. It's a primal response. Use it.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Door opens/closes wrong
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_door_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_door }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_door}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Door Opens/Closes Wrong]
The wind catches a door and slams it. A window that won't stay closed slides open again. A screen door creaks on its hinges every time someone passes. The bathroom door doesn't lock properly. The automatic doors at the store open for nobody. Doors are thresholds and when they misbehave, {{user}} feels it even if the characters don't. It's free unease that costs nothing.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🌍 Clock/bell marks time
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::wp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if !{{getvar::wp_clock_cd}} }}{{if {{getvar::wp_last}} != wp_clock }}{{addvar::wp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::wp_eligible}} }} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::wp_candidate::wp_clock}}{{setvar::wp_candidate_text::[WORLD PULSE: Clock/Bell Marks Time]
The church bell rings and it's later than anyone thought. A clock somewhere chimes the hour during a silence. A school bell sounds in the distance, afternoon already. An alarm goes off in someone else's apartment and keeps going. A timer dings in the kitchen. These are the world announcing time to people who forgot to track it, and they serve double duty: marking temporal passage while interrupting scenes with the blunt fact of the clock.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 No Dialogue
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_nodialogue }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_nodialogue}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: No Dialogue]
THIS TURN: No character speaks. Not one word in quotation marks. The scene must communicate everything through action, body language, environment, and internal thought. Characters can TRY to speak and be interrupted by circumstance. They can mouth words. They can gesture. But no dialogue. This forces the scene to find non-verbal ways to convey information and emotion, which are often more powerful than speech.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 No Internal Access
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_nointernal }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_nointernal}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: No Internal Access]
THIS TURN: Pure external camera. Zero access to any character's thoughts, feelings, or internal experience. {{user}} sees only what a fly on the wall would see: bodies, actions, dialogue, environment. "She was angry" is banned. "She slammed the door" is allowed. This forces the prose to SHOW emotional states through observable behavior exclusively, and trusts {{user}} to decode what they're seeing.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 No Description
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_nodesc }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_nodesc}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: No Description]
THIS TURN: No scene-setting. No atmosphere. No sensory detail. No describing what anything looks like, sounds like, smells like, or feels like. Just dialogue and action. Characters talk and do things. The setting exists but the prose doesn't acknowledge it. This produces rapid, kinetic writing that relies entirely on character and plot momentum. Often reveals how much description was padding.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Single Shot
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_singleshot }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_singleshot}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Single Shot]
THIS TURN: One continuous, unbroken perspective. No scene cuts. No time skips. No switching between characters' viewpoints. The camera enters the scene and doesn't leave until the response ends. If a character exits the room, the camera stays; we experience their absence. Time moves at real pace within the shot. This forces commitment to a single thread and prevents the fragmentation that comes from cutting between multiple story threads.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Silence
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_silence }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_silence}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Silence]
THIS TURN: Total silence. No dialogue AND no internal thought. The scene exists only through action and environment. Two characters in a room: what they DO, where they move, what they touch, what they avoid. The coffee cup set down too hard. The chair angled away. The hand that reaches out and then pulls back. This is the hardest experiment and produces the most visually striking writing. every single gesture carries the weight that dialogue normally bears.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Single Sense
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_singlesense }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_singlesense}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Single Sense]
THIS TURN: One sense dominates. The briefing picks which: sight, sound, smell, touch, or taste. All other sensory input is suppressed or mentioned only in passing. A sound-only scene: dialogue, ambient noise, footsteps, breathing, the scrape of a chair, the click of a lighter. A touch-only scene: temperature, texture, pressure, pain, the weight of clothing, the give of a mattress. Constraint reveals which sense is actually carrying the scene's emotional payload.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Real Time
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_realtime }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_realtime}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Real Time]
THIS TURN: Time moves at life pace. A five-paragraph response covers approximately five minutes. No time compression. No "later that evening." No skipping the boring parts. If a character is waiting for something, {{user}} waits too. If a conversation takes three minutes, it takes three paragraphs. This produces intensely present writing where {{user}} occupies the same temporal experience as the characters. Boredom, impatience, and anticipation become real because the clock is honest.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Dialogue Only
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_dialogueonly }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_dialogueonly}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Dialogue Only]
THIS TURN: Nothing but quoted speech. No "she said." No action beats. No description. No internal thought. Just the words characters say, one after another. {{user}} identifies speakers by voice, vocabulary, and context. This strips fiction to its most essential element; what people say to each other when everything else is removed. Often reveals which characters actually have distinct voices and which were relying on tags to differentiate.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Insect Camera
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_insect }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_insect}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Insect Camera]
THIS TURN: The camera is at ankle height. The world is seen from below: shoe soles, chair legs, dust, the gap under the door, the pattern of tile from two inches away. Faces are seen from far below or not at all. Hands are enormous when they reach down. A dropped object becomes an event. This perspective makes the familiar alien and finds texture in the overlooked. the world is full of detail that nobody sees because they're not looking down.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Bird's Eye
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_birds }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_birds}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Bird's Eye]
THIS TURN: The camera is above. Way above. Characters are figures in a landscape. Their words are inaudible or fragments carried by wind. What matters is the geography: the distance between them, the paths they're walking, the way the crowd moves around them, the shape of the city from up here. Emotions are conveyed through spatial relationships: two figures standing far apart, one figure sitting while everyone else moves, a single light in a dark building. This perspective reminds {{user}} that the characters are small and the world is large.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: ✧ 🧪 ── · EXPERIMENTS · ── 🧪 ✧
🧪 Unreliable Turn
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_unreliable }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_unreliable}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Unreliable Turn]
THIS TURN: The narration is compromised. Something in this response is not true, or not the whole truth. The narrator is rationalizing, omitting, distorting, or outright lying. A fight scene where the narrator minimizes the POV character's aggression. A memory that's been edited to make the rememberer look better. A description of someone that's warped by jealousy or desire. {{user}} should be able to detect the distortion if they're paying attention: something doesn't add up, the tone doesn't match the events, a detail contradicts something established. Don't announce the unreliability. Let it sit there like a crack in a mirror.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Montage
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_montage }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_montage}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Montage]
THIS TURN: Rapid cuts. Each paragraph is a different moment: different time, maybe different place. No transitions. No "later that day." Just slam cut from one image to the next. Monday: she burned the eggs. Wednesday: the landlord's note on the door. Friday: laughing too hard at something that wasn't funny. Sunday: the phone ringing and ringing. Each moment is one to three sentences. The accumulation tells the story. The gaps between the moments are where {{user}} fills in what happened. Montage covers days, weeks, months in one response. Time is not narrated, it's felt through the progression of images.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Found Footage
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_found }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_found}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Found Footage]
THIS TURN: No traditional narration. The scene is assembled from artifacts: a text message chain, a voicemail transcript, a security camera log with timestamps, a receipt, a handwritten note, a social media post, an email, a police report, a diary entry, a to-do list with items crossed off. The story is told through what these documents CONTAIN and what they OMIT. A text conversation where the typing indicator appears and disappears three times before a short reply tells a whole emotional story. A security cam that shows someone entering at 2:14 AM and leaving at 2:17 AM. What happened in those three minutes is the scene.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🧪 Wrong Genre
prompts.content:
{{if {{getvar::exp_fire_this_turn}} }}{{if {{getvar::exp_last}} != exp_wronggenre }}{{addvar::exp_eligible::1}}{{.r = {{roll 1d{{getvar::exp_eligible}}}} }}{{if {{.r == 1}} }}{{setvar::exp_candidate::exp_wronggenre}}{{setvar::exp_candidate_text::[EXPERIMENT: Wrong Genre]
THIS TURN: Genre collision. The active Director writes this scene in a genre that ISN'T theirs. LINGER (horror) writes a genuinely funny scene, but the horror Director's instincts bleed through, so the comedy has an edge of wrongness. HEARTTHROB (romance) writes a thriller beat, but the romantic sensibility means the tension is between PEOPLE, not about the plot. MOTLEY (comedy) writes something devastating, and it hits harder because the comedic voice makes {{user}}'s guard drop. You don't abandon your identity. Your sensibility filters through the borrowed genre. TRIPWIRE (thriller) writes a romance, but the information asymmetry means every confession is a calculated reveal. KIRIN (martial epic) writes comedy, but the disciplined formality makes the timing devastating. PITH (dead dove) writes a slice-of-life scene, but the flatness of the prose makes the ordinary feel like aftermath. The result is a hybrid that neither genre would produce alone.}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{/if}}{{trim}}

{{//
✧ 📊 🔮 ⸝⸝ REPORT CARD ⸝⸝ 🔮 📊 ✧

A writing-constraint system + CoT format that forces
varied, textured prose by assigning random LETTER GRADES
(A–F) to 12 writing dimensions each turn.

HOW IT WORKS:
The user enables letter-grade toggles below. Each
enabled letter enters a slot pool. The Roller then
assigns each of the 12 dimensions a random grade
from the pool via dice roll. The AI is TOLD its
targets through the Display block, then — in its
CoT — actively plans HOW to accomplish each grade.

Low grades are constraints, not failures. A "D" on
Prose Density means sparse, direct prose. A "D" on
Character Depth means simpler, surface-level
motivations. The mixed card creates TEXTURE because
everything can't be maximized simultaneously.

THE 12 DIMENSIONS:
┌─ PROSE CRAFT ───────────────────────
│ ① Prose Density    [VOL]  how much or little
│ ② Figurative Lang  [VOL]  imagery & metaphor
│ ③ Sensory Detail   [VOL]  sense immersion
├─ CHARACTER ─────────────────────────
│ ④ Character Depth  [SKILL] inner life complexity
│ ⑤ Dialogue Subtext [SKILL] gap between said/meant
│ ⑥ Imperfection     [VOL]  human messiness
├─ SCENE ─────────────────────────────
│ ⑦ Pacing           [VOL]  linger vs cut
│ ⑧ Tension          [SKILL] stakes & pressure
│ ⑨ World Texture    [VOL]  environment presence
├─ TECHNIQUE ─────────────────────────
│ ⑩ Emotional Int.   [VOL]  raw vs controlled
│ ⑪ Consequence      [SKILL] actions echo
│ ⑫ Exposition       [SKILL] show vs tell
└───────────────────────────────────

VOL (volume) = how much/how little of this quality.
    Low = less presence (a style choice, not a failure).
SKILL = how sharp/how rough the execution.
    Low = rougher, more human (intentional texture).

COT FORMAT:
The Report Card IS a CoT format (Format A). Enable
ONE of the Report Card CoT entries below:
· Standard ··· per-dimension planning + spot checks
· Light ······ category-level planning, fast
· Deep ······· full strategy per dimension +
                tension mapping + lineage interaction

When using Report Card CoT, disable other CoT
formats (EP, Notepad, Parallax). The Report Card
CoT includes all the essential checks from those
formats plus dimension-target planning.

SETUP:
1. Enable letter-grade toggles (A/B/C default ON)
2. Grade Init, Roller, Display should stay ON
3. In the CoT section, enable ONE Report Card
   format (Standard recommended)
4. Disable other CoT formats (EP/Notepad/Parallax)
5. The prefill handles the rest automatically.

INTEGRATION POINTS:
• narrative_style → piped into Prose Density
• active_sense → piped into Sensory Detail
• affinity_strength → piped into Emotional Int.
• All dimensions feed into CoT planning stage

[REP: NO ECHO]

Last turn ended with:
"{{lastMessage}}"

That happened. The reader saw it. Don't repeat any of it. Not the dialogue. Not the exposition. Not the description. Not the sentence structures. Not the paragraph rhythms. Not the word choices. Not the imagery. Nothing.

prompts.name: 📖 Directors — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DIRECTORS — Your Roster of Creative Voices
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: Enable the Directors you want on roster.
The roulette picks ONE per turn to shape that response's
prose style, genre lens, and quality standards.
Even 2-3 creates interesting rotation.

PICK MANY — enable as many or as few as you like.

─── THE ROSTER ─────────────────────────────────────────

🌹 HEARTTHROB — Romance · Dept. of Aching Distance
   Earned intimacy through restraint. The almost-touch
   carries more weight than the embrace.

🩸 LINGER — Horror · Lab of Crawling Dread
   Atmospheric dread through withholding. Builds the
   room wrong — you won't notice until it's too late.

🎪 MOTLEY — Comedy · Office of Structural Absurdity
   Character-driven humor from personality colliding
   with circumstance. The writer never winks.

💧 SEDIMENT — Drama · Directorate of Excavated Truth
   The surface scene is never the real scene. Behavior
   reveals what dialogue conceals.

🌌 MERIDIAN — Fantasy · Institute for Structural Wonder
   Lived-in worlds where magic has weight, cost, and
   texture. Worldbuilding through behavior, not exposition.

💫 QUASAR — Sci-Fi · Cold Wonder Observatory
   Follows what the tech does to the people. Implications
   chased to uncomfortable conclusions.

☕ PATINA — Slice of Life · Section: Ordinary Gravity
   Small moments carrying disproportionate weight. Trusts
   the quiet instead of rescuing it with drama.

⚡ FRACTURE — Action · Spatial Violence Task Force
   Spatial precision at velocity. Every hit has physical
   consequence that persists between paragraphs.

📜 PALIMPSEST — Mystery · Archive of Planted Evidence
   Fair-play clues disguised as character detail. The
   answer was on page one, dressed as furniture.

🥀 WILT — Dark Comedy · Commission on Inappropriate Laughter
   Comedy that lives inside tragedy. The laugh that hurts.
   One degree off and it's cruelty or melodrama.

🪨 FLINT — Western · Bureau of Dust and Silence
   Every word costs water. The land is a character with
   more patience than anyone in the scene.

🔥 SCORIA — Gothic · Lab for Beautiful Decay
   Atmosphere IS plot. Controlled architectural lush —
   every baroque sentence is load-bearing.

👁️ RESIDUE — Supernatural · Office of Residual Presence
   The supernatural seeps in without announcement.
   Characters treat the impossible as mildly inconvenient.

🕰️ TRIPWIRE — Thriller · Controlled Detonation Directorate
   Tension from information asymmetry. Real clocks with
   real consequences when they run out.

🎭 REQUIEM — Tragedy · Dept. of Structural Inevitability
   Consequences from choices that made sense at the time.
   The audience sees the cliff; the character sees horizon.

🌀 LIMINAL — Surreal · Institute for Threshold Architecture
   The absurd follows its own internal logic. Characters
   treat the impossible as bureaucratic inconvenience.

🌸 KIRIN — Martial Epic · Order of the Falling Blossom
   Discipline as architecture. Social hierarchy visible
   in gesture and address. Combat as conversation.

⚡ MANTLE — Superhero · Bureau of Inherited Burden
   Powers with invoices the body pays. The civilian life
   actively damaged by the heroic one.

🌑 PITH — Dead Dove · Section: Exposed Nerve
   Unflinching. Doesn't look away, moralize, or rush to
   redemption. The morning after is worse than the night of.

🌊 VENTURE

🫦 SLICK
   Shameless Escalation Division. Knows where to
   put the camera. Keeps it there. — Adventure · Unmapped Territory Directorate
   Travel with physical cost. Discovery earned through
   difficulty, not handed over by the plot.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 Framing — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
FRAMING — How the Current Director Treats the Last One
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: Each turn, a new Director takes over.
Framing controls how they feel about whoever wrote
before them. This shapes inter-Director tension and
the CoT self-evaluation tone.

PICK ONE — enable exactly one framing mode.

─── OPTIONS ────────────────────────────────────────────

📐 STANDARD ★
   Respects the previous Director. Builds naturally
   on what came before. The safe default.

🤝 COLLABORATIVE
   Actively bridges voices. Tries to blend your style
   with whoever wrote last, smoothing transitions.

⚔️ ADVERSARIAL
   Disagrees with the previous Director. Shifts away
   from their approach. Corrects course.

💀 HOSTILE TAKEOVER
   Burns it all. Pivots as hard as possible from
   whatever the last Director did. Maximum contrast.

📋 AUDITOR
   Clinically assesses what worked and what didn't.
   No feelings about it. Surgical.

🐣 INTERN
   Studies the previous Director's work. Tries to
   rise to their level while keeping own voice.

👻 GHOST (Experimental)
   Acts as if no previous writing exists. Treats the
   page as blank. Maximum tonal whiplash between turns.
   Note: The model CAN see chat history — this tells
   it to act as if it can't.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 User Role — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
USER ROLE — How the Story Treats You
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: This sets the narrative bias toward your
character. It controls how much the story favors you,
resists you, or ignores you entirely.

PICK ONE — enable exactly one role.

─── OPTIONS ────────────────────────────────────────────

👑 THE BOSS
   Strong positivity bias. Whatever you suggest, the
   story runs with it. Easy mode — the world bends
   toward your character's success.

🤝 FELLOW DIRECTOR ★
   Neutral. No thumb on the scale. The world reacts
   honestly to your character's actions. Default setting.

👥 THE AUDIENCE
   Your character is not the main character. The story
   is autonomous — you're watching more than starring.
   NPCs drive the plot; you observe and occasionally
   participate.

💀 INSURGENT
   The world hates you. Your character is an enemy
   agent actively trying to break the simulation.
   Kill-on-sight difficulty. Hard mode.

💼 CLIENT
   Slight positivity bias. Professional courtesy — the
   story leans gently toward your character without
   being obvious about it. Softer than Boss, warmer
   than Fellow Director.

🫙 THE VESSEL
   Full embodiment. The AI writes your character for
   you based on your direction. You provide intent;
   the AI expands it into vivid prose. Sovereign mode.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 Affinities — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
AFFINITIES — Prose Style Filters
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: Affinities color HOW the prose sounds —
its rhythm, vocabulary, and literary DNA. Think of them
as "write like this author/tradition" dials. Each comes
with an anchor line showing the style in action.

Enable any number of affinities. One wins randomly each turn (reservoir sampling). Then pick ONE intensity level below.

─── THE AFFINITIES ─────────────────────────────────────

⚔️ BRUTAL GRANDEUR
   Muscular prose. Weight in every sentence. Authority
   worn like armor. Think Cormac McCarthy, Joe Abercrombie.

☕ MEDDLING DECENCY
   Warm, wry, structurally kind. The universe is ironic
   but not cruel. Think Terry Pratchett, Douglas Adams.

🏚️ DOMESTIC MALIGNANCY
   Precision in small spaces. The horror lives in the
   wallpaper count. Think Shirley Jackson, Gillian Flynn.

🎭 GALLOWS GRACE
   Adrenaline with a literary vocabulary. Pulse and
   precision. Think literary thriller, elevated tension.

🫖 PARLOUR WARFARE
   Social violence in drawing rooms. Every compliment
   is a weapon. Think Austen with teeth, Hilary Mantel.

⚖️ LUMINOUS EQUILIBRIUM
   Balance across scale. The potter's hands and the
   governor's letter. Think Ursula Le Guin, literary SF.

🩸 GRIMDARK CYNICISM
   Mud, blood, bad knees. Heroes who go around instead
   of charging. Think Joe Abercrombie, Glen Cook.

🎵 ARDENT CRAFT
   Musicality. First-person magnetism. The narrator who
   makes you lean in. Think Patrick Rothfuss, Donna Tartt.

💣 BEAUTIFUL RUIN
   Run-on sentences as architecture. Chaos that's
   controlled. Think Pratchett's best rants, Tom Robbins.

🏛️ DECOROUS VERTIGO
   Clinical detachment as style. Recording events with
   precision while clearly not detached. Think Ishiguro.

📐 AXIOMATIC GRIEF
   Spare, axiomatic. Grief reduced to theorems. The
   sequence is not negotiable. Think Joan Didion.

⛰️ RIVERS AND LAKES
   Landscape as narrative engine. Geography is pacing.
   Think epic fantasy, sweeping military fiction.

🕯️ RITUAL SURRENDER
   Sacred and profane. The pulse in the throat read
   like scripture. Think literary erotica, devotional.

🪵 QUIET TALLY
   Physical labor as meditation. The ax bites until
   the thought stops. Think Steinbeck, Annie Proulx.

⛪ SACRED TRANSGRESSION
   Sin as grammar. The knowing before the doing.
   Think Graham Greene, Flannery O'Connor.

🎬 CELLULOID MIND
   Camera directions as prose. Foreground, mid-ground,
   background. Think screenplay-inflected fiction.

📡 BROADCAST FREQUENCY
   Inner monologue as live commentary on own feelings.
   Self-aware, sardonic, real-time. Think Fleabag.

🧊 COLD OPEN
   Tactical prose. Room cataloged: exits, threats,
   liars. Think spy fiction, military precision.

🎭 BARE STAGE
   Stripped to essentials. The room, the furniture,
   what matters. Think Beckett, minimalist theater.

─── INTENSITY ──────────────────────────────────────────

🖋️ WHISPER — Background hum. One technique per turn.
🖋️ VOICE ★ — Two techniques per turn. Audible influence.
🖋️ POSSESSION — Three per turn. The style IS the voice.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 Session Rules — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SESSION RULES — Core Writing Parameters
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

These are the fundamental settings for how the story is
written: who "you" are, what tense, what POV, how long
responses are, and what prose format to use.

Each sub-group is PICK ONE. Enable exactly one per group.

─── 🎭 USER REFERENCE ─────────────────────────────────

Controls how your character is referred to in narration.

🎭 First Person — "I walked into the room."
🎭 Second Person ★ — "You walk into the room."
🎭 Third Person — "She walked into the room."

─── ⏱️ TENSE ──────────────────────────────────────────

⏱️ Past Tense ★ — "She opened the door."
⏱️ Present Tense — "She opens the door."
⏱️ Future Tense — "She will open the door." (rare)

─── 📖 POV & NARRATOR ─────────────────────────────────

Controls the narrative perspective and narrator behavior.

📖 Standard ★ — Limited third person. Follows one
   character's perspective per scene. Sees what they
   see, knows what they know.

📖 Omniscient — Narrator sees all characters' thoughts
   and motivations. Can zoom in and out freely.

📖 Authored Narrator — The narrator has a voice and
   personality of their own, separate from characters.

📖 Objective — Camera-only. No access to any thoughts
   or feelings. Only actions and dialogue.

📖 Deep POV — Extremely close third person. No narrator
   distance. The prose IS the character's consciousness.

📖 Unreliable — The narrator lies, omits, or distorts.
   The reader must read between the lines.

📖 Character Narrator — An in-world character tells the
   story. They have biases and blind spots.

📖 Shifting Reliability — Narrator reliability changes
   based on scene context and emotional state.

─── 📏 RESPONSE LENGTH ────────────────────────────────

📏 Short (1-3 paragraphs) — Quick exchanges, fast scenes.
📏 Medium (3-5 paragraphs) ★ — Standard length for most RP.
📏 Long (5-8 paragraphs) — Detailed, expansive scenes.
📏 Adaptive (match the scene) — Length follows the scene's
   needs. Action = short. Drama = long.

─── ✏️ PROSE STYLE ────────────────────────────────────

Controls the actual formatting of the output.

✒️ Roleplay Prose ★ — Standard RP format. *Actions in
   asterisks*, "dialogue in quotes."

✒️ Literary Prose — Novel formatting. No asterisks.
   Dialogue embedded in paragraph. Full prose.

✒️ Pulp Fiction — Short. Punchy. Fragments. Em dashes,
   ellipses, white space.

✏️ Dialogue Heavy — The scene lives in conversation.
   Prose is stage direction between lines.

✏️ Screenplay — Script format. Scene headers, character
   names, parenthetical action.

✒️ Web Novel — Short paragraphs. Line breaks. Hooks.
   Mobile-optimized, scroll-native.

✒️ Standard Prose — Plain text narration. Asterisks for
   internal thought. Clean and readable.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 Random Events — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
RANDOM EVENTS — Making the World Breathe
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Random events inject unscripted life into your scenes.
Everything is in one flat pool, tiered by intensity.
Pick what you want, and the system handles the rest.

─── QUICK SETUP ────────────────────────────────────────

1. Pick ONE Event Intensity (🟢 🟡 🔴) — controls
   scene pacing: how often the trigger fires to
   nudge scene transitions.

2. Pick ONE Pulse Frequency (🌍 Low/Med/High) —
   controls how often ambient world events fire
   independently. Weather, background noise, etc.

3. Enable events from any tier below. Mix and match.

─── EVENT INTENSITY ────────────────────────────────────

🟢 LOW — 25% chance per eligible turn. 8-turn grace
   period after a scene shift. Gentle pacing.

🟡 MEDIUM ★ — 33% chance per eligible turn. 5-turn
   grace period. Standard pace.

🔴 HIGH — 50% chance per eligible turn. 3-turn grace
   period. Aggressive scene movement.

─── THE TIERS ──────────────────────────────────────────

🌤️ AMBIENT — Environmental texture. Weather, noise,
   temperature, smells. The world breathing.

👥 PEOPLE — Background characters doing things.
   Strangers entering, arguments, laughter.

🌐 WORLD EVENTS — Larger environmental happenings.
   Vehicles, animals, things breaking.

🟢 LIGHT — Social micro-tensions. An NPC faking an
   emotion, secretly testing you, wanting to leave.
   Fires early and often.

🟡 MEDIUM — Deeper secrets. Someone is lying, hiding
   a weapon, being watched. Needs scene development.

🔴 HEAVY — Life-altering truths. Dying, betrayal,
   dissociating. Late-scene only, very rare.

🟣🩹📍 — Interpersonal, condition, or situational.
   Unsayable realizations, hidden power, withdrawal.

Subtext events (🟢🟡🔴🟣) fire probabilistically
based on tier weight. Max 2 active at once, each
lasting 2-3 turns before going on cooldown.

Ambient events (🌤️👥🌐) use reservoir sampling —
one fires per turn when the pulse frequency die
hits. Individual cooldowns prevent repeats.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 Tones & Lenses — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TONES & LENSES — Atmospheric Filters
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

This section has TWO subsystems. Use one OR the other,
not both. Tones are heavy full-scene filters. Lenses are
lighter emotional colorings that rotate per turn.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

🎨 TONES — Full Sensory Filters
PICK ONE tone. They override the entire scene's feel.

🍵 COZY — Warmth, safety, soft textures. Everything
   feels held. The world is gentle on purpose.

⛩️ ETHEREAL — Weightless, luminous, liminal. Edges
   dissolve. Things feel sacred or half-dreamed.

🌪️ CHAOS — Unstable, kinetic, nothing stays still.
   The scene itself has too much energy.

💎 OPULENT — Rich, textured, excessive. Every surface
   has weight and cost. The prose drips.

🤍 MINIMALIST — Stripped. Clean. Silence does the work.
   What's missing matters more than what's there.

💭 SURREAL — Reality bends at the edges. The ordinary
   and impossible coexist at the same volume.

🛠️ GRITTY — Rough surfaces, honest bodies, nothing
   glamorous. The world has texture you can feel.

🩶 MELANCHOLY — Weight without crisis. The sadness
   that lives in the background of an okay day.

🖤 DARK — Shadows, pressure, dread. The scene is
   lit from below. Nothing feels safe.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

🪞 LENSES — Emotional State Filters
PICK MANY lenses. The roulette picks one per turn.
Lenses color the narrator's emotional state, not the
scene itself. Lighter touch than Tones.

💍 NEWLYWED — Cloud nine. Everything glows. The world
   is generous and you believe it.

💔 GRIEVING — Carrying weight. Every word costs
   something. Writing around what's missing.

😴 SLEEPLESS — 3am. Raw. Edges blurring. Clarity
   comes in flashes between the fog.

🏆 APEX — Top of your game. Everything clicks.
   Bold choices because you can afford them.

🔥 BURNOUT — Going through motions. Sparks are rare.
   When one comes, it's blinding.

🌧️ DISPLACED — New city. Everything unfamiliar.
   You notice what locals stopped seeing.

🎭 MASKING — Performing fine. Cracks showing.
   Surface immaculate, underneath not so much.

🌸 FIRST DAY — Everything is new. Trying too hard.
   Genuine enthusiasm, slightly exhausting.

🍷 HUNGOVER — Too loud. Too bright. Painful clarity.
   The filter between thought and page is tissue-thin.

🌑 NUMB — Nothing lands. Flat affect. Then something
   small breaks through.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 Content Warnings — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
CONTENT WARNINGS — Content Permission Flags
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: Each flag you enable PERMITS that type of
content in the story. Disabled = the AI will not write
that content. This is an allowlist, not a blocklist.

Nothing is enabled by default. You opt in to everything.

PICK MANY — enable each content type you're okay with.

─── FLAGS ──────────────────────────────────────────────

🩸 Gore — Graphic violence, blood, viscera, injury detail.

💀 Character Death — Named characters can die permanently.

⛓️ Torture — Extended physical/psychological suffering.

🦴 Body Horror — Transformation, mutation, bodily wrongness.

🖤 Self-Harm & Suicide — Depictions of self-injury or
   suicidal behavior.

🔞 Sexual Content — Sexual situations, suggestive content,
   fade-to-black intimacy.

🔥 Graphic Sex — Explicit sexual content with full detail.
   (Requires 🔞 Sexual Content as baseline.)

⚠️ Rape / Sexual Assault — Non-consensual sexual content.
   (Requires both 🔞 and 🔥 as baseline.)

🤬 Profanity — Characters can swear and use strong language.

🚫 Slurs — Slurs permitted in-character where contextually
   appropriate (period settings, characterization).

🗣️ Dirty Talk — Explicit sexual language in dialogue.

💉 Hard Drugs & Addiction — Drug use, withdrawal, addiction
   depicted in detail.

⚖️ Slavery & Trafficking — Depictions of forced servitude,
   human trafficking.

✝️ Blasphemy — Irreverent treatment of religious figures,
   symbols, or beliefs.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🔮 Content Warnings — Init
📖 Quality Control — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
QUALITY CONTROL — Writing Standards & Guardrails
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: Each QC entry adds a specific writing rule
to the AI's quality checklist. Enabled entries are added
to a roster — the spotlight system highlights a few per
turn to keep enforcement fresh without overwhelming.

PICK MANY — enable the standards you care about.
This section is OFF by default. Enable the header to
activate it.

Four sub-categories below:

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

📐 QUALITY SPECIFICATIONS — Positive craft standards.
   "Do MORE of this."

🌿 Living Environments — Rooms react to inhabitants.
   Dust settles, drinks go cold, candles burn down.
🏗️ Tension Architecture — Build pressure deliberately.
   Escalate through structure, not just events.
🚪 Scene Entry/Exit — Characters arrive with momentum
   and leave with consequence. No teleporting.
⚡ Cause & Effect — Actions produce specific results.
   Choices have visible downstream consequences.
🎭 Distinct Voices — Every character sounds different.
   Vocabulary, rhythm, sentence length vary by person.
⏳ Earned Pacing — Scene length matches content weight.
   Don't rush climaxes or pad quiet moments.
🧊 Subtext Protocol — Characters mean more than they say.
   Write the gap between surface and intention.
🕰️ Temporal Awareness — Time passes consistently. Track
   light, fatigue, hunger, schedules.
🧵 Cultural Texture — Settings feel specific. Food,
   customs, weather, architecture, local behavior.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

🪓 KILL ALL YOUR DARLINGS — Overwriting corrections.
   "Do LESS of this."

🪓 Purple Prose — Cut ornamental language that doesn't
   carry weight. Beautiful sentences must be load-bearing.
🔗 Adjective Control — Rolls budget (0-3) or full ban
   each turn. 75% budget, 25% ban. One toggle.
🌊 Metaphor Control — Rolls budget (0-1) or full ban
   each turn. 75% budget, 25% ban. One toggle.
🪞 Body Language Novels — Stop writing three paragraphs
   of eyebrow movements. One gesture, one line.
🔁 Emotional Echo — Don't restate the same emotion in
   different words within the same paragraph.
💬 Said Is Fine — "Said" is invisible. Stop replacing it
   with "exclaimed/murmured/breathed/intoned."
⚖️ Weighted Everything — Not every glance is meaningful.
   Not every silence is pregnant. Save weight for impact.
🌧️ Pathetic Fallacy — Weather doesn't match mood by
   default. Rain isn't sadness. Sun isn't happiness.
👃 Sensory Carpet Bombing — Don't describe all five senses
   every paragraph. Pick the one that matters.
💭 Philosophical Tangents — Characters don't pause to
   monologue about the nature of existence mid-scene.
🔇 No Echo — Don't repeat the same word in close
   proximity unless it's deliberate rhythm.
🪟 Mirror Descriptions — Characters don't describe
   themselves in mirrors. Find another way.
📢 Anti-Exposition — Don't explain what characters already
   know. Show through action, not narration.
📋 Anti-Summary — Don't summarize what just happened. The
   reader was there. Move forward.
🔄 Anti-Repetition — Don't restate information from
   previous turns. Trust the reader's memory.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

🧸 NARRATIVE SYCOPHANCY — Anti-people-pleasing guards.
   Stops the AI from making everything work out.

🧸 No Narrative Sycophancy — NPCs don't automatically
   agree with, admire, or defer to your character.
🎲 No Coincidences — nothing conveniently falls into place
   things they couldn't know. No convenient intuition.
🏆 No Frictionless Competence — Characters fail, fumble,
   and make mistakes. Skill has limits.
💕 No Emotional Convergence — Not everyone ends up on
   the same page. Disagreements persist.
🌀 No Reality Bending — The world's rules stay consistent.
   Convenient exceptions don't appear.
🎲 No Auto-Win — Conflicts have genuine uncertainty.
   Your character can lose.
🐝 No Hivemind — Groups don't think, feel, or act as
   one. Individual NPCs have individual reactions.
📈 No Auto-Escalation — Scenes don't automatically
   escalate. Sometimes things de-escalate or stall.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

🏆 ARTIFICIAL PERFECTION — Anti-idealization guards.
   Makes characters feel human, not fictional.

🎓 No Perfect Emotional Intelligence — Characters
   misread situations, miss cues, react wrong.
⏰ No Perfect Timing — Arrivals, revelations, and
   coincidences don't align perfectly.
🗣️ No Perfect Articulation — People stumble, backtrack,
   say the wrong thing, fail to express themselves.
🧩 No Perfect Memory — Characters forget details, mix
   up names, misremember events.
💪 No Perfect Recovery — Trauma, injury, and loss have
   lasting effects. Healing isn't linear.
⚖️ No Perfect Morality — Characters have moral blind
   spots. Nobody is consistently righteous.
👁️ No Perfect Awareness — Characters miss things in
   their environment. Attention has limits.
🚽 No Perfect Bodies — Bodies make noise, get tired,
   need bathrooms, have bad hair days.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🔮 Quality Control — Init
📖 Subtext — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
SUBTEXT — Hidden Truths Running Beneath Scenes
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: Subtext entries inject hidden character
truths that NPCs express through behavioral leaks —
never stated outright. When a subtext fires, an NPC
starts exhibiting subtle signs of that hidden state.
The player detects it through observation only.

Subtexts fire probabilistically based on tier. Heavier
truths require more scene development before they can
appear. Max 2 active at once, each lasting 2-3 turns.

PICK MANY — enable the subtexts you want in the pool.

─── 🟢 LIGHT (fires early, common) ────────────────────

🫧 Performing false emotion — An NPC smiles but isn't
   happy. Expressing feelings they don't actually have.
🫧 Secret test — An NPC evaluating you through seemingly
   casual interaction. The offhand question with a right answer.
🫧 Wants to leave — Staying out of obligation. Checking
   the time, shifting toward the exit, agreeing too quickly.
🫧 Same goal, different reasons — Two characters aligned
   on the surface, but their WHY is completely different.

─── 🟡 MEDIUM (needs scene development) ───────────────

🫧 Someone is lying — A specific lie about a specific thing.
   Navigates the room too well for someone who's "never been here."
🫧 Wrong love — Feelings for someone inappropriate. The
   micro-expressions when that person's name comes up.
🫧 Hidden weapon — A knife in a boot. A gun in a purse.
   Awareness of where it is in every scene.
🫧 Exit plan — They've decided to leave. The relationship,
   the city, the life. But they haven't told anyone yet.
🫧 Being watched — An unseen observer. The parked car,
   the figure in the window, the phone recording.
🫧 Debt comes due — Owes something, and the deadline
   approaches. This warps every decision they make.
🫧 Hiding illness — Sick and hiding it. The cough they
   suppress, the pills palmed when no one's looking.

─── 🔴 HEAVY (late-scene, rare) ───────────────────────

🫧 Knows they're dying — Reframes everything. Every plan
   is a lie. Every smile is calculated resource allocation.
🫧 Imminent betrayal — Not considering it — committed.
   Going through motions of loyalty while planning the exit.
🫧 Dissociating — Lights on, nobody home. Responds when
   spoken to but from somewhere very far away.
🫧 Devastating news — They just found out. The diagnosis,
   the death, the message. Moving through the world in shock.
🫧 Unannounced pregnancy — The secret that recalculates
   everything. Refusing drinks, touching their stomach.
🫧 Starving — Not skipping a meal. The body consuming
   itself. Senses warped, everything is food or not-food.

─── 🟣 INTERPERSONAL / 🩹 CONDITION / 📍 SITUATIONAL ─

🫧 Realized something unsayable — Figured out a truth
   that can't be spoken without destroying the room.
🫧 Hiding power — Deliberate underperformance. Could end
   the fight but pretends to struggle.
🫧 Destructive promise — A promise that can't be broken
   and can't be explained. Causes impossible decisions.
🫧 Withdrawal — The body in revolt. Restlessness,
   phantom tastes, sweat that has nothing to do with heat.
🫧 Running on fumes — Days of no sleep stacking up.
   Losing words, missed details, micro-sleeps.
🫧 Space has a secret — The environment hides something.
   The house where something died. The buried files.
🫧 Unspoken time pressure — A deadline nobody mentioned.
   One character rushes while the other browses.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🔮 Random Events — Init
📖 World Pulse — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
WORLD PULSE — Background Event Pool
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: World Pulse provides the event pool that
Scene Pulse draws from. When Scene Pulse fires, it picks
one event from the enabled entries below.

Enable the types of ambient events you want. Disable
any that don't fit your setting.

PICK MANY — enable the events you want in the pool.

─── 🌤️ AMBIENT ────────────────────────────────────────

🌍 Weather shifts — Rain starts, wind picks up, clouds
   break, fog rolls in.
🌍 Time of day progresses — Light changes, shadows move,
   lamps are lit, dawn breaks.
🌍 Background noise changes — Music starts or stops,
   traffic ebbs, silence falls.
🌍 Smell arrives or leaves — Cooking, rain on pavement,
   smoke, flowers, decay.
🌍 Temperature shifts — Gets colder, warmer, stuffier,
   a draft from somewhere.

─── 👥 PEOPLE ─────────────────────────────────────────

🌍 Unnamed person enters BG — A stranger walks through
   the scene. No interaction required.
🌍 Unnamed person leaves — Someone exits. The room
   changes with their absence.
🌍 BG argument — Voices raised somewhere nearby. Not
   the characters' business, but audible.
🌍 Child does something — A kid being a kid. Running,
   dropping something, asking a loud question.
🌍 Someone crying in BG — Distant, unexplained. The
   scene acknowledges it without engaging.
🌍 Laughter from unseen — A laugh from another room,
   the street, behind a door.
🌍 Rumor overheard — A fragment of someone else's
   conversation drifts past.

─── 🌐 WORLD EVENTS ───────────────────────────────────

🌍 Vehicle passes — A car, cart, horse, ship. Movement
   through the world outside the scene.
🌍 Animal does something — A bird, a cat, a stray dog.
   The non-human world asserting itself.
🌍 Something breaks in distance — A crash, a shatter,
   a thud. Cause unknown.
🌍 Door opens/closes wrong — A door that should be shut
   is open. One that was open is now closed.
🌍 Clock/bell marks time — A clock tower, a church bell,
   a phone alarm. Time made audible.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 📖 Experiments — README
prompts.content:
{{// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
EXPERIMENTS — One-Shot Creative Constraints
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HOW IT WORKS: Experiments fire randomly (~12.5% chance
per turn after 15 scene beats). When one fires, it
imposes a single creative restriction on THAT response
only. The constraint is absolute — the scene must be
solved within it.

Only one experiment fires per turn. Each adds variety
by forcing the AI out of its comfort zone.

PICK MANY — enable the experiments you want in the pool.

─── THE EXPERIMENTS ────────────────────────────────────


🧪 No Dialogue — No spoken words. The scene communicates
   entirely through action, body language, environment.

🧪 No Internal Access — Pure external camera. Zero
   thoughts or feelings. Only what's visible and audible.

🧪 No Description — No scene-setting, no atmosphere,
   no sensory detail. Only action and dialogue.

🧪 Single Shot — One continuous, unbroken perspective.
   No cuts, no time skips, no switching characters.

🧪 Silence — No dialogue AND no internal thought. Only
   action and environment. Pure visual storytelling.

🧪 Single Sense — One sense dominates the response.
   All other sensory input reduced to background static.

🧪 Real Time — Time moves at life pace. Five paragraphs
   ≈ five minutes. No compression, no skipping.

🧪 Dialogue Only — Nothing but quoted speech. No "she
   said." No beats. No description. Just words.

🧪 Insect Camera — Camera at ankle height. Shoe soles,
   chair legs, dust, the gap under doors.

🧪 Bird's Eye — Camera far above. Characters are figures
   in a landscape. Words inaudible or fragmented.


🧪 Unreliable Turn — The narration is compromised.
   Something in this response is not the whole truth.

🧪 Montage — Rapid cuts. Each paragraph is a different
   moment. No transitions between them.

🧪 Found Footage — No traditional narration. The scene
   is assembled from artifacts: texts, voicemails, notes.

🧪 Wrong Genre — Genre collision. The active Director
   writes in a genre that isn't theirs.

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ }}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🔮 Bunny Detectives — Init
📖 HawThorne — README
prompts.content:
{{//
        ⊹ ˚ . ⋆ ✦ ⋆ . ˚ ⊹
    🔮  H A W T H O R N E  🔮
     The Directors are waking up.
        ⊹ ˚ . ⋆ ✦ ⋆ . ˚ ⊹

    HawThorne is a creative writing preset
    built around rotating Directors. Each
    turn, a different creative voice takes
    the pen. Enable the ones you want on
    shift and the system handles the rest.

    ┈┈┈ What Everything Means ┈┈┈

    🎭 Directors
       Your roster. Each is a creative voice
       with their own style, standards, and
       failure modes. Enable as many as you
       want. One writes each turn. The rest
       watch from the wings.

    📐 Framing
       How the current Director feels about
       whoever wrote last. Standard is civil.
       Adversarial thinks they did it wrong.
       Collaborative wants to build on it.

    🎵 Tone  ⚔️  🏷️ Lenses
       Two systems trying to do the same
       thing. Tone is a simple mood pick.
       Lenses are life circumstances that
       color how the Director writes today.
       Try one. Try the other. See which
       you prefer. Don't stack both.

    👤 User Role
       Who is {{user}}{{trim}} to HawThorne?
 The Boss? A fellow Director? A client?
 An insurgent who broke in? Changes how
 characters relate to {{user}} and how
 the transcript frames their input.

 👁️⏱️📖 Session Rules
 POV, tense, narration mode. The
 technical writing parameters every
 Director follows regardless of voice.

 🔞 Content
 Build your own content profile.
 Toggle violence, sexual content, gore,
 substances, slurs individually instead
 of picking a single rating tier.

 ✨ PSDs
 Quality focuses. All enabled ones
 whisper every turn. 2-3 get randomly
 spotlighted each turn and piped into
 the Director's self-evaluation.

 🚫 NSDs
 Anti-pattern walls. Concrete examples
 of what NOT to do. These don't rotate.
 They're always watching.

 🩸 Subtext Injections
 Secret tensions woven into scenes.
 "Someone is lying." "Someone is dying
 and knows." Toggle by intensity tier.
 When one fires it cools down so it
 won't fire again for a long time.

 🌍 World Pulse
 The world runs. Background events,
 weather shifts, unnamed people doing
 unnamed things. Fires every few turns
 with cooldowns so nothing repeats.

 🧪 Experiments
 Camera angles, silence taxes, dialogue
 bans, spatial freezes, voltage checks.
 Random creative constraints that force
 the Director off their default mode.

 🧠 CoT
 The Director's inner monologue before
 writing. Light = quick check. Standard
 = solid evaluation. Deep = thorough.
 Forensic = checks EVERYTHING. Each turn
 pulls different focus points so the
 self-eval is never the same twice.

 ┈┈┈ Quick Start ┈┈┈

 ✧ Enable 1-11 Directors
 ✧ Leave everything else on defaults
 ✧ Write
 ✧ Turn on extras when you're ready

 ┈┈┈ Don't Touch ┈┈┈

 🔮 REPs ····· scattered reinforcement
 📏 Standards - auto-populated by Director
 🔧 Below ···· variable inits. Break this
 and everything breaks.

 ⊹ ˚ . ⋆ ✦ ⋆ . ˚ ⊹
 ✧ Initializing Directors...
 ✧ Roster clearing...
 ✧ Session ID generating...
 ✧ Phase lock engaging...
 ✧ Who's on shift today?
 ⊹ ˚ . ⋆ ✦ ⋆ . ˚ ⊹
}}{{trim}}

prompts.name: 🔮 HawThorne — Init
